message: Coffee's cold already. Look - we're doing jalapeño corn dog bites this week. Hear me out before you panic.
Actually wait, are we leaning into the heat or playing it safe with the breading?
The heat needs to matter - if we're doing this, we lean in. Half measures just make it taste like regret.
The breading's gotta have some bite to it then - maybe a chipotle-dusted coating so the heat builds instead of just hitting you at the end?
The chipotle dust creates a flavor arc - mild at first bite, then the smoke catches up with you - so the copy needs to track that progression instead of just listing ingredients.
That progression angle actually works. Chipotle dust, build the heat, let the copy follow the experience. We're making something that tastes like it means something. See you tomorrow.
